text:
Team GB cyclist Tom Pidcock was roundly jeered aftersealing Team GB's second gold medal of the 2024 Paris Olympics. In a thrilling finale to the cross-country mountain bike race, Pidcock came into contact with Frenchman Victor Koretzky as he pulled off a thrilling overtaking manoeuvre at the end of the final lap.

And the home crowd reacted angrily, booing Pidcock as he celebrated gold.

Not that the 24-year-old seemed bothered as he celebrated his dramatic win, after somehow overturning a 39-second deficit between him and Korentzky.

Pick up your ultimate guide to the Olympics! Going for Gold provides the day-by-day rundown of what's on and when. Clickhereto buy.

While leading the race on the third lap, Pidcock suffered a puncture which looked to have scuppered his chances. But he dug deep to mount a magnificent fightback, with British fans left angry at the lack of the respect from those in attendance.

"Unfortunately he was booed by the crowd at the finishing line, not good" wrote @Liklesis on X. "The French spectators should've been more respectful, it's a shame they forgot themselves in the heat of the moment."



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

The crowd did at least remain respectful during the medal ceremony as God Save the King was played. And Pidcock himself later defended his gold medal winning move, arguing he was entitled to attack the space his rival had left.

"He left a gap and I had to go for it," he told theBBC. "But that's racing, some people might view it differently. But I want to show what sport is, not giving up. It's a shame the French were booing me, but you know.

"I knew that Victor was going to be super fast on the last lap, even if I couldn't get rid of him. So I knew it was going to be a big big fight."

Don't miss:Brazilian swimmer issues emotional statement after being sent home from Olympics

Don't miss:Team GB hero 'wished she wasn't British' and fights back tears at Olympics 2024

Don't miss:What is in the cardboard boxes handed to Olympic medallists on podiums in Paris

He also admitted to feeling emotional after his win. "It wasn't easy," he said. I'm keeping my glasses on for a reason. I think the Olympics is so special, you never give up and you have to give everything, that's what I had to do."

Pidcock's win signified a second gold medal in Paris for Team GB. Just hours earlier, the equestrian team of Laura Collett, Tom McEwen and Ros Cantertriumphed in the three-day eventing.

title: Team GB Olympics gold medallist booed after thrilling mountain bike comeback triumph | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fraser Watson
description: Team GB have won a second gold medal at the Paris Olympics.
